title: CRISPR Clinical Trials: A 2025 Update - Intellia in Focus
text: The 2025 update on CRISPR clinical trials highlights Intellia Therapeutics as a leading innovator in in vivo gene editing. Intellia's NTLA-2001 (nex-z) is the first investigational CRISPR therapy to be administered systemically to edit genes inside the human body. The treatment is made of CRISPR components encased in lipid nanoparticle (LNPs) and delivered in a single dose by IV with the LNPs accumulating in the liver. For transthyretin (ATTR) amyloidosis, nex-z showed the ability to reduce TTR by more than 85% to 90% after a single dose in Phase 1 data. For hereditary angioedema (HAE), Intellia's lonvo-z (formerly NTLA-2002) advanced to Phase 3 with positive clinical outcomes. Intellia's Phase 3 clinical trial for its CRISPR-based gene therapy enrolled first patients, advancing the company's mission to develop potentially curative therapeutics using CRISPR/Cas9-based technologies.
